title: Witch Week - Chapter 14 (Fragment 1)
text:
14

14

M R. WENTWORTH OPENED the door and ushered the four of them into the classroom, into a blast of stares and whispers. “I’m afraid I had to kidnap these four,” Mr. Wentworth said to Mr. Crossley, who happened to be teaching the rest. “We’ve been arranging my study for the inquisitor to use.”

Mr. Crossley seemed to believe this without question. 6B, to judge from their faces, felt it was an awful letdown. They had expected all four of them to have been arrested. But they made the best of it.

“Mr. Towers is looking for you two,” Simon whispered righteously to Nirupam and Charles. And Theresa said to Estelle, “Miss Phillips wants you.” Nan was lucky. Miss Phillips never remembered Nan if she could help it.

They had arrived back so late that there was only a short piece of lesson left before lunchtime. When the bell rang for lunch, Charles and Nirupam kept to the thickest crowds. Neither of them wanted Mr. Towers to see them. But Charles had his usual bad luck. Mr. Towers was on duty at the door of the dining hall. Charles was very relieved when he slipped past without Mr. Towers showing any interest in him at all.

Nirupam nudged Charles as they sat down after grace. Chrestomanci was sitting beside Miss Cadwallader at high table, looking bland and vague. Everyone craned to look at him. Word went around that this was the divisional inquisitor.

“I don’t fancy getting on the wrong side of him,” Dan Smith observed. “You can see that sleepy look’s just there to fool you.”

“He looks feeble,” said Simon. “I’m not going to let him scare me.”

Charles craned to look too. He knew what Simon meant, but he was quite sure by now that Chrestomanci’s vague look was as deceptive as Dan thought. Mr. Wentworth was up at high table too. Charles wondered where Brian was and how Brian would get any lunch.

Charles turned back to the table to hear Theresa saying, “He is so super looking, he makes me feel quite weak!”

To everyone’s surprise, Estelle jumped to her feet and leaned across the table, glaring at Theresa. “Theresa Mullett!” she said. “You just dare be in love with the inquisitor and see what you get! He’s mine. I met him first and I love him! So you just dare!”

Nobody said a word for a moment. Theresa was too astonished even to giggle. Everyone was so unused to seeing Estelle so fierce that even the monitor in charge could not think what to say.

During the silence, it became clear how Brian was going to get lunch. Charles and Nirupam felt themselves being pushed apart by an invisible body. Both of them were jabbed by invisible elbows as the body climbed onto the bench between them and sat. “You’ll have to let me eat off your plates,” whispered Brian’s voice. “I hope it’s not stew.”

Luckily, Simon broke the silence just as Brian spoke. He said, in a jeering, not-quite-believing way, “And what took you all so long to arrange for Mr. Feeble Inquisitor?”

From the way everyone looked then, Nan knew nobody had believed Mr. Wentworth’s excuse for an instant. She could see most of them suspected something like the truth. Help! thought Nan. “Well we had to put a lot of electric wiring into the study,” she invented hastily. “He has to have a bright light arranged to shine into people’s faces. It helps break them down.”

vocabulary.definition: to organize or make plans for something
vocabulary.example: “We’ve been arranging my study for the inquisitor to use.

vocabulary.definition: a sudden intense wave of people staring and whispering
vocabulary.example: WENTWORTH OPENED the door and ushered the four of them into the classroom, into a blast of stares and whispers.

vocabulary.definition: to cause something or someone to lose resistance or to analyze into parts
vocabulary.example: Nirupam nudged Charles as they sat down after grace.

vocabulary.definition: a light that shines strongly
vocabulary.example: “He has to have a bright light arranged to shine into people’s faces.

vocabulary.definition: to stretch out one's neck in order to see better
vocabulary.example: Everyone craned to look at him.

vocabulary.definition: making you believe something that is not true
vocabulary.example: He knew what Simon meant, but he was quite sure by now that Chrestomanci’s vague look was as deceptive as Dan thought.

vocabulary.definition: a system of electrical cables and connections
vocabulary.example: “Well we had to put a lot of electric wiring into the study,” she invented hastily.

vocabulary.definition: very weak or lacking strength
vocabulary.example:
”

“He looks feeble,” said Simon.

vocabulary.definition: showing strong emotion or aggression
vocabulary.example: Everyone was so unused to seeing Estelle so fierce that even the monitor in charge could not think what to say.

vocabulary.definition: staring in an angry or fierce way
vocabulary.example:
”

To everyone’s surprise, Estelle jumped to her feet and leaned across the table, glaring at Theresa.

vocabulary.definition: in a hurry, quickly but without taking care
vocabulary.example: “Well we had to put a lot of electric wiring into the study,” she invented hastily.

vocabulary.definition: a person who asks questions in an aggressive or formal way, sometimes to find out information
vocabulary.example: “We’ve been arranging my study for the inquisitor to use.

vocabulary.definition: a body that cannot be seen but is perceived
vocabulary.example: Charles and Nirupam felt themselves being pushed apart by an invisible body.

vocabulary.definition: expressing scorn or mockery
vocabulary.example: He said, in a jeering, not-quite-believing way, “And what took you all so long to arrange for Mr.

vocabulary.definition: a disappointment or failure to meet expectations
vocabulary.example: 6B, to judge from their faces, felt it was an awful letdown.

vocabulary.definition: the time of day when people usually eat lunch
vocabulary.example: When the bell rang for lunch, Charles and Nirupam kept to the thickest crowds.

vocabulary.definition: a person who supervises others, often children or students
vocabulary.example: Everyone was so unused to seeing Estelle so fierce that even the monitor in charge could not think what to say.

vocabulary.definition: feeling happy that something unpleasant has not happened or has ended
vocabulary.example: Charles was very relieved when he slipped past without Mr.

vocabulary.definition: in a morally right or justifiable way
vocabulary.example: Towers is looking for you two,” Simon whispered righteously to Nirupam and Charles.

vocabulary.definition: to move quietly and quickly past someone or something
vocabulary.example: Charles was very relieved when he slipped past without Mr.

vocabulary.definition: a room used for reading, writing, or academic work
vocabulary.example: “We’ve been arranging my study for the inquisitor to use.

vocabulary.definition: appearing very attractive or impressive
vocabulary.example: Towers is looking for you two,” Simon whispered righteously to Nirupam and Charles.

vocabulary.definition: to guide someone somewhere, especially by showing them the way
vocabulary.example: WENTWORTH OPENED the door and ushered the four of them into the classroom, into a blast of stares and whispers.
